level_signals: Expected to set direction, influence engineering leadership, and drive org-wide alignment
Comp range $168K-$252K base consistent with Lead/Staff IC level
'Proven experience as a Lead or Senior Platform Product Manager'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Proven experience as a Lead or Senior Platform Product Manager in data infrastructure, distributed systems, or developer platform environments — with a track record of driving velocity and reliability at scale.
Deep understanding of data architecture and large-scale systems; experience with event-driven systems, high-volume ingestion pipelines, or complex data movement patterns.
Comfort engaging in highly technical discussions around pipelines, infrastructure, and system evolution; ability to operate at both strategic and highly detailed technical levels.
Understanding of the transformative potential of AI in platform engineering; ability to separate hype from practical value and drive adoption of AI-forward practices.
Fluency in measuring success via engineering productivity, quality, and reliability metrics; data-driven approach to prioritization and investment decisions.
Proven ability to define structure and clarity in ambiguous, newly forming teams; skilled at stakeholder management across multiple technical organizations.
Thrives in fast-moving environments; balances short-term wins with long-term strategy and biases toward automation, simplicity, and self- service solutions.
Please note this role requires 5 days/week in our Boston oﬃce.

scope.surface: Data Ingestion Platform — ingestion, transformation, and reliability of large-volume data within Klaviyo's core systems
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS (marketing automation / ecommerce data platform)
core_responsibilities: Define the long-term mission, charter, and ownership boundaries for a newly formed team
Develop and maintain a coherent, high-impact roadmap balancing foundational investments with active initiatives
Define the strategy for ingesting large volumes of data from numerous sources
Partner with engineering to design ingestion systems that are scalable, resilient, and maintainable
Institutionalize AI and automation as force multipliers across ingestion workflows
Establish instrumentation and success metrics to validate impact and adjust priorities
Drive improvements in observability, monitoring, and operational excellence for ingestion systems
Identify and drive architectural simplifications that improve platform health
Create transparency and open feedback loops with engineers, engineering managers, and senior leadership

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and wide ($168K-$252K base)
Clear product surface and ownership area (Data Ingestion Platform for a newly formed team)
Explicit success criteria stated in JD
Role has genuine strategic scope — defining vision for a newly formed team, not just executing
Company explicitly states openness to close-but-not-exact matches
red_flags: 5 days/week in Boston office required — fully eliminates remote and hybrid candidates
No years of experience minimum explicitly stated, creating ambiguity about seniority bar
Newly formed team with 'ambiguity' — may lack established processes, tooling, or organizational support

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from role title 'Lead PM', JD language about setting direction/influencing engineering leadership, and comp range)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as B2B SaaS ecommerce marketing platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and success criteria sections; specific targets not stated)
ambiguities: No explicit minimum years of experience stated; 'Lead or Senior Platform PM' language implies substantial seniority but no numeric floor given.
Visa sponsorship and work authorization requirements are entirely absent from the JD.
Nice-to-haves section is absent from the JD — all requirements appear in a single 'What You Bring' section framed as expected qualifications; all treated as hard must-haves.
